questions.text: I believe that the drive to achieve personal goals is one of the healthiest motivations.
I can handle multiple stressors simultaneously without feeling destabilized.
I hold the view that overthinking social signals leads to unnecessary confusion.
I am energized by the act of making a consequential choice.
I feel that planning for worst-case scenarios wastes energy better spent on execution.
I naturally speak first in group discussions.
I experience the world through a lens of emotional vividness that rarely dims.
I notice when someone changes the subject to avoid an uncomfortable topic.
I believe I have a talent for bringing people together around a shared purpose.
I hold the view that every spare resource I have should flow toward those who need it.
I maintain consistent sleep and wake times because I value the stability they provide.
I believe that approaching emotionally complex situations with analytical rigor is the most reliable way to ensure fair treatment for everyone.
I feel most aligned with my values when I am serving a purpose bigger than myself.
I believe that suffering, wherever it occurs, creates moral claims that are binding regardless of personal convenience.
I feel that when suffering is present, its moral claim on me is immediate and does not require further justification.
I am aware of the invisible alliances that form in any group setting.
I find that my emotional reactions are louder and more persistent than I'd choose if I could choose.
I feel that a structured approach to problem-solving leads to more reliable outcomes.
